title: schiedea perlmanii
intro: Schiedea perlmanii, also known as the Ka'u Island schiedea, is a rare and endangered flowering plant endemic to the Ka'u Desert on the Big Island of Hawaii. It is a succulent plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. Schiedea perlmanii has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Schiedea perlmanii include infusions and tinctures, and it is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function.
